LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS INDUSTRIAL CREW, LLC & GOLDEN GATE DISPOSAL & RECYCLING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION AS TO GENERAL NEGLIGENCE AND PREMISES LIABILITY. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT MET THEIR BURDEN OF ESTABLISHING THAT DEFENDANTS OWED A LEGAL DUTY TO PLAINTIFFS SINGLY OR COLLECTIVELY. IT IS UNDISPUTED IN THIS CASE, THAT DEFENDANTS WERE MORE THAN JUST PROVIDERS OF INFORMATION. DEFENDANTS WERE HIRED BY PG&E TO CONDUCT REPAIRS AND MAINTENANCE ON THE COMPACTOR AND SHOULD HAVE FORSEEN THAT FAILURE TO REPAIR AN ALLEGED LEAK WOULD RESULT TO WORKERS BEING EXPOSED TO AN INJURY. THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED AS UNOPPOSED. THE COURT RULED ON THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER PURSUANT TO CCP 437 C(G) AND CRC 3.1312). LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WHICH WAS NOT CONTESTED. DEFENDANTS INDUSTRIAL CREW, LLC AND GOLDEN GATE DISPOSAL & RECYCLING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. DEFENDANTS DO NOT PRESENT SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE ESTABLISHING THAT THE TECH-BILT FACTORS ARE MET HERE. CONCLUSORY ALLEGATIONS AS TO THE SETTLING PARTY'S LIABILITY ARE INSUFFICIENT. (GRESHKO V. COUNTY OF LOS ANGELES (1987) 194 CAL.APP.3D 822, 834.) FURTHER, SUBSTANTIAL EVIDENCE SHOWING THE NATURE AND EXTENT OF THE SETTLING DEFENDANT'S LIABILITY IS NECESSARY AND REQUIRED; WITHOUT SUCH EVIDENCE, A DETERMINATION OF GOOD FAITH IS AN ABUSE OF DISCRETION. (MATTCO FORGE, INC. V. ARHUR YOUNG & CO. (1995) 38 CAL.APP.4TH 1337, 1348.) (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER THAT COMPLIES WITH CCP 437C(G) AND CRC 3.1312.) JUDGE: ERNEST H. Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement;
Off Calendar May-20-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: CHRISTOPHER JOHNSON, ESQ., 818.587.9146, FOR DEFENDANTS INDUSTRIAL CREW, LLC AND GOLDEN GATE DISPOSAL & RECYCLING COMPANY.
RAYMOND MEYER JR, ESQ., 510.840.4881, FOR DEFENDANT PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTs INDUSTRIAL CREW, LLC and GOLDEN GATE DISPOSAL & RECYCLING COMPANY's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement is granted. PG&E has not met its burden to show that the settlement was not made in good faith. (City of Grand Terrace v. Superior Court (1987) 192 Cal.App.3d 1251, 1261.) The settlement is within ballpark based on the Settling Defendants' proportionate liability for Plaintiff's fall. The evidence presented by PG&E is insufficient to negate Plaintiff and Plaintiff's employer's potential comparative negligence or to establish that the settlement figure is "grossly disproportionate" to a reasonable estimate of the Settling Defendants' liability. (Tech-Bilt, Inc. v. Woodward-Clyde & Associates (38 Cal.3d 488, 499.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Joyce Finley #8620.

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement;

Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR# 5976.

Notice Of Motion And Moton For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement;
Off Calendar Mar-12-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling which was not contested. DEFENDANTs INDUSTRIAL CREW, LLC and GOLDEN GATE DISPOSAL & RECYCLING COMPANY's Motion For Determination Of Good Faith Settlement is denied without prejudice. Defendants do not present sufficient evidence establishing that the Tech-Bilt factors are met here. Conclusory allegations as to the settling party's liability are insufficient. (Greshko v. County of Los Angeles (1987) 194 Cal.App.3d 822, 834.) Further, substantial evidence showing the nature and extent of the settling defendant's liability is necessary and required; without such evidence, a determination of good faith is an abuse of discretion. (Mattco Forge, Inc. v. Arhur Young & Co. (1995) 38 Cal.App.4th 1337, 1348.) (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order that complies with CCP 437c(g) and CRC 3.1312.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976.

RULING - A R G U E D; DEFENDANTS INDUSTRIAL CREW, LLC & GOLDEN GATE DISPOSAL & RECYCLING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION - MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS DENIED AS TO THE CAUSES OF ACTION AS TO GENERAL NEGLIGENCE AND PREMISES LIABILITY. DEFENDANTS HAVE NOT MET THEIR BURDEN OF ESTABLISHING THAT DEFENDANTS OWED A LEGAL DUTY TO PLAINTIFFS SINGLY OR COLLECTIVELY. IT IS UNDISPUTED IN THIS CASE, THAT DEFENDANTS WERE MORE THAN JUST PROVIDERS OF INFORMATION. DEFENDANTS WERE HIRED BY PG&E TO CONDUCT REPAIRS AND MAINTENANCE ON THE COMPACTOR AND SHOULD HAVE FORSEEN THAT FAILURE TO REPAIR AN ALLEGED LEAK WOULD RESULT TO WORKERS BEING EXPOSED TO AN INJURY. THE REQUEST FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE IS GRANTED AS UNOPPOSED. THE COURT RULED ON THE EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order pursuant to CCP 437 C(g) AND CRC 3.1312).
